market_summary: As of Sunday, March 22, 2026, the U.S. cash market is closed, so this plan is for Monday, March 23, 2026. Friday's tape closed with elevated stress as the VIX finished at 26.78, QQQ materially underperformed SPY, and market coverage stayed focused on oil-driven inflation pressure, tariff uncertainty, and renewed AI/tech de-risking. The best tactical opportunities are selective energy longs that align with sector strength and one tactical semiconductor short if growth selling resumes after the open.

XOM.execution_condition: Only buy if XOM holds above 159.50 and then trades through 160.50 after the first 15 minutes while crude-sensitive names remain stronger than SPY.
XOM.reasoning: Energy was one of the few clear pockets of relative strength into the weekend as oil-sensitive equities outperformed while broader indexes weakened. Exxon also closed near the top of its daily range on strong volume, which fits a continuation setup better than trying to buy weaker growth sectors in a risk-off regime.
catalysts: Oil-linked inflation narrative keeping energy leadership intact
Relative sector strength versus broad market weakness
risks: Crude reverses lower and energy leadership fades after the open
Broad index liquidation overwhelms sector-relative strength

supporting_articles.title: More pain at the pump: average US price of gas jumps 19 cents, biggest weekly increase since May 2024
supporting_articles.reason: Supports the oil and refined-product strength that underpins the energy-long thesis.

supporting_articles.title: Europe shares climb at the open led by oil stocks
supporting_articles.reason: Confirms cross-market leadership from oil-sensitive equities rather than speculative tech.

CVX.execution_condition: Only buy if CVX holds above 200.50 and reclaims 202.50 with volume expansion while XLE stays green versus SPY.
CVX.reasoning: Chevron showed the same defensive-commodity leadership profile as Exxon and benefits from the same inflation-and-oil narrative. In this environment, buying a high-liquidity integrated producer is more consistent with the regime than chasing beaten-down AI or consumer discretionary names.
catalysts: Strength in integrated oils as energy outperforms
risks: Oil/gas headlines cool off and sector momentum stalls
Failed breakout above 202.50 traps late buyers

supporting_articles.title: More pain at the pump: average US price of gas jumps 19 cents, biggest weekly increase since May 2024
supporting_articles.reason: Reinforces the inflationary energy backdrop supporting major integrated producers.

supporting_articles.title: Europe shares climb at the open led by oil stocks
supporting_articles.reason: Provides evidence that oil stocks were among the clearest global leadership groups heading into Monday.

JPM.execution_condition: Only act if JPM clears 288.00 on rising volume and XLF continues to outperform SPY intraday.
JPM.reasoning: Financials held up materially better than technology on Friday, but the group is not as clean a leadership theme as energy. JPM remains liquid and institutionally sponsored, so it is worth monitoring for relative-strength continuation rather than forcing a pre-open long.
catalysts: Value rotation away from high-duration tech
Relative strength in financials versus QQQ-heavy growth
risks: Higher volatility broadens into all cyclicals including banks
Rates move lower and erase the sector rotation tailwind

supporting_articles.title: Wall Street ends lower as Trump tariffs, inflation test market recovery
supporting_articles.reason: Frames the macro tape driving rotation toward sturdier balance-sheet sectors.

supporting_articles.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall to end volatile month as AI worries buffet markets
supporting_articles.reason: Highlights the growth-led weakness that could keep money rotating into financials.

NVDA.execution_condition: Only short if NVDA loses 172.00 early, fails to reclaim VWAP in the first hour, and QQQ remains under pressure.
NVDA.reasoning: Semiconductor and AI leadership fractured again into the weekend, and Nvidia closed weak on very heavy volume while broad-market volatility stayed elevated. In a risk-off tape, this is the cleaner downside expression than trying to short defensive sectors.
catalysts: Growth-stock de-risking in a high-VIX environment
risks: Fast oversold rebound in semis squeezes shorts
Any positive AI headline reverses the opening weakness

supporting_articles.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall to end volatile month as AI worries buffet markets
supporting_articles.reason: Directly ties Nvidia and the Nasdaq weakness to the current market narrative.

supporting_articles.title: Wall Street ends lower as Trump tariffs, inflation test market recovery
supporting_articles.reason: Supports the broader risk-off tape that increases downside follow-through risk in semiconductors.

CCL.execution_condition: Only buy if CCL breaks above 24.40 after earnings and holds that level for at least 15 minutes with volume materially above Friday's pace.
CCL.reasoning: Carnival has a clear catalyst with earnings this week, but discretionary travel is not the area to press longs before the market proves it wants risk. This stays on watch because a strong report can still overpower the weak macro tape for a tactical trade.
risks: Weak consumer/risk appetite punishes discretionary names
Post-earnings gap fails and fades into market weakness

supporting_articles.title: Upcoming Earnings This Week: Carnival, Chewy, Braze and More
supporting_articles.reason: Confirms Carnival as a scheduled catalyst name for the coming week.

CHWY.execution_condition: Only buy if CHWY reclaims 23.90 after earnings and holds above VWAP while the consumer cohort is not lagging the tape.
CHWY.reasoning: Chewy has an earnings catalyst, but it closed weak into the event and does not align with the current defensive-commodity leadership. This is a conditional watch for a confirmed post-earnings reversal, not a preemptive long.
catalysts: Potential margin or guidance upside surprise
risks: Miss or cautious guidance triggers another leg down
Low-conviction consumer tape limits bounce quality

supporting_articles.title: Upcoming Earnings This Week: Carnival, Chewy, Braze and More
supporting_articles.reason: Confirms Chewy as a near-term catalyst name but not yet a confirmed trade.

BRZE.execution_condition: Only buy if BRZE clears 18.60 after earnings and holds above that breakout level with broad software selling contained.
BRZE.reasoning: Braze has a discrete earnings catalyst, but software remains vulnerable when rates and volatility rise together. The setup is only attractive if earnings create a clean momentum break that can overcome the weak regime backdrop.
catalysts: Potential guidance reset that sparks software rotation
risks: Software remains a funding source in a risk-off tape
Post-earnings move lacks liquidity and quickly reverses

supporting_articles.title: Upcoming Earnings This Week: Carnival, Chewy, Braze and More
supporting_articles.reason: Identifies Braze as a catalyst-driven watchlist candidate for the week ahead.

NATL.execution_condition: Only consider NATL if the stock stays above 42.20 and deal-related news keeps the spread stable rather than widening.
NATL.reasoning: NCR Atleos is event-driven after the Brinks acquisition announcement, which gives it a defined catalyst but a less attractive risk/reward than the energy setups. It remains on watch as a special-situations name, not a priority directional trade.
catalysts: Brinks acquisition announcement for NCR Atleos
risks: Merger-arbitrage spread widens on execution concerns
Liquidity is thinner than the primary focus names

supporting_articles.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall to end volatile month as AI worries buffet markets
supporting_articles.reason: This live market report highlighted NCR Atleos as a notable premarket mover tied to a cash-and-stock acquisition.
